title: The Winners, Losers, and WTFs of the Golden Globes
format: Sean and Amanda break down a chaotic, Zoom-bound Golden Globes: the HFPA's overdue diversity reckoning, David Fincher's drinking game, and how Nomadland and Andra Day snuck past the favorites.

segments.heading: The HFPA Under the Microscope
segments.note: the LA Times exposé, Tina Fey's cold open, and a suspiciously well-timed run of Black winners

films.title: Judas and the Black Messiah
films.note: Daniel Kaluuya's Best Supporting Actor win: his mic cut out the moment he started speaking, an accidental metaphor for the whole telecast.

films.title: Soul
films.note: Co-director Kemp Powers wasn't told he'd been nominated ahead of the show; the HFPA appears to have only started crediting co-directors in the days before the ceremony, the same rule that left Coco's Adrian Molina uncredited a few years back.

segments.heading: Nobody Watched the Movies They're Introducing
segments.note: the clip-package ritual, and what happens when almost nobody's seen the nominee

films.title: Music
films.note: Kate Hudson, its star, had to introduce it herself. Nobody else who'd seen the film could be found.

films.title: The Father
films.note: Colin Farrell did the honors from a room the hosts call his 'murder room,' complete with a stray bongo drum.

segments.heading: Best Speeches of the Night
segments.note: Lee Isaac Chung's win with his daughter at his side

films.title: Minari
films.note: Won Best Foreign Language Film, boxed out of the bigger categories by the same rule that limited Parasite the year before. Chung's speech about the language of family is the moment both hosts single out.

films.title: Borat Subsequent Moviefilm
films.note: Won Best Musical or Comedy and got Cohen the acting prize in the same category; his 'thank you to the all-white HFPA' line was, per the hosts, the funniest of the night.

segments.heading: Jodie Foster's Surprise, and the Aaron Rodgers of It All

films.title: The Mauritanian
films.note: Foster's Best Supporting Actress win was one of the night's bigger surprises; her speech thanked co-star Shailene Woodley's newly announced fiancé, Aaron Rodgers, which the hosts spend a while unpacking.

segments.heading: David Fincher Takes a Shot Every Time He Loses

films.title: Mank
films.note: Up for Best Director, Best Drama, and Best Screenplay (written by Fincher's late father, Jack) and won nothing. The hosts clock him drinking on camera after every loss.

segments.heading: Aaron Sorkin's Screenplay Win Goes Sideways

films.title: The Trial of the Chicago 7
films.note: Sorkin's Best Screenplay win, predicted by the hosts, comes with a speech they call patronizing: he seems to be addressing the wrong nominee, Emerald Fennell, while talking up his daughter.

segments.heading: A Wide-Open Best Actress Race
segments.note: both Best Actress prizes went a way nobody predicted

films.title: I Care a Lot
films.note: Rosamund Pike's Best Actress, Musical or Comedy win. The hosts think the film itself is mediocre but note the punny title has become its own meme.

films.title: The United States vs. Billie Holiday
films.note: Andra Day's Best Actress, Drama win was the night's biggest surprise. Neither host had heard anyone predict it, and both credit Day's own singing as the reason the performance works.

films.title: Nomadland
films.note: The favorite over The Trial of the Chicago 7; the hosts read the win as part of a bigger Netflix sweep across the night's film and TV categories.

segments.heading: Mailbag: Is Music the Worst Globes Nominee Ever?
segments.note: a listener asks Sean and Amanda to rank it against history's worst nominees

films.title: Bobby
films.note: Sean's comparison for a genuinely bad-but-explicable nominee: Emilio Estevez's star-studded, mediocre film about the hours before RFK's assassination, also a Best Drama nominee in its year.

segments.heading: Up Next
segments.note: the two episodes teased at the close

films.title: Promising Young Woman
films.note: Teased as next week's episode; Carey Mulligan's Best Actress, Drama nomination went unrewarded tonight.

films.title: Coming 2 America
films.note: The episode after that: an Eddie Murphy career retrospective ahead of the sequel's release.

films: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
